rulings.Case Title: MARGERY JAQUITH HOLLISTER VS. GREGORY OVALLE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner?S Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 26, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER MARGERY HOLLISTER Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment is GRANTED. Arbitration Award is confirmed in its entirety. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA VS. ANNA NADELSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 26, 2021 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA, VOLHA NAHORNAYA DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to October 1, 2021. In sustaining the prior demurrer, the Court ordered the cross-complaint to attach an English translation of the contract per CRC 3.1110(g). All causes of action alleged in this complaint are based in part on the allegation that Mr. Yakimchuk moved-in without prior knowledge/consent of the owner and/or that the intent to move Mr. Yakimchuk in was concealed from the owner. This allegation is contradicted by the language of the contract, which cross-complainant seeks to enforce: "At the end of the summer, you informed me of your circumstances in the family by asking to let you for a couple of months a second room for your newly arrived sister and her husband. Taking into account your circumstances and after discussing your situation with the members of my family, we decided to satisfy your request and allowed temporary dwelling for your sister and her husband." Cross-Complainant now claims that there is some "translation discrepancy" and that the contract "does not unequivocally state that Cross-Complainant was informed that. Mr. Yakimchuk would be moving in at the time. It merely states that as of December 2018, Mr. Yakimchuk was residing at the Property." (Opposition 5:23-28). By no later than September 24, 2021 cross-complaint to file a translation of the contract without "translation discrepanc[ies]" or provide a declaration that the translation attached to the complaint is the proper, code complaint translation of the contract attached to the second amended cross-complaint as an exhibit. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FONGKOK ZHOU ET AL VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 26, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF FONGKOK ZHOU, SHAO ZHOU, JACK ZHOU MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION is DENIED. On June 24, 2021 the Court heard a demurrer to the First Amended Complaint. On that demurrer Defendant asked for judicial notice of the Settlement Agreement in prior litigation. Plaintiff did not object to the request for judicial notice. Therefore, in rendering its decision the Court considered the four corners of the complaint (which included no reference to the settlement, it effect or enforceability) and judicially noticeable documents (no objection) and their legal effect. Based on the review of appropriate filings on the demurrer, the Court allowed the Plaintiff to amend the complaint to plead around (in good faith) the provisions of the settlement agreement. Instead of doing so, Plaintiff filed this motion for reconsideration asking the Court to look at the extrinsic evidence (including multiple declarations) that could not have been properly considered on a demurrer to determine that the settlement agreement does not preclude the causes of action asserted in the First Amended Complaint. Even if the Court were to find that there are new facts, law or circumstances warranting reconsideration, the Court would not be able to consider any of the evidentiary arguments (e.g. intent, etc.) on its new re-considered demurrer ruling. All of these assertions shall be made in good faith in an amended complaint, as already authorized and ordered by the Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN KOURY VS. MATTHEW GRAHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 26, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT MATTHEW GRAHAM DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to September 10, 2021. By no later than September 1, 2021 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief clarifying who is recovering possession: (1) manager/signatory to the lease in his individual capacity pursuant to an agreement with the owner; or (2) trustee, in his capacity as the owner of the property; or (3) both. If Plaintiffs claim that both he is able to seek possession in both of these capacities Plaintiff shall cite authorities for the proposition that an unlawful detainer action may be brought by an individual in his capacity as a manager/signatory to the lease and separately in his capacity as the trustee of the owner Trust. By no later than September 7, 2021 Defendant may file a response to the supplemental brief. By no later than September 1, 2021 Defendant may submit a certified copy of the Trust Transfer Deed. By no later than September 7, 2021 Plaintiff may respond to the arguments based on judicial notice of the Trust Transfer Deed identifying Samir Khoury as Trustee of the owner Trust. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
